logs.name: worn-raisin
logs.text:
>Attempting suicide isn't similar to "actual" suicide? Good lord, that's a new one.

I know it's crazy how I get hung up on trivial little differences like somebody dying. 

>The only real difference is method success rate.

That sentence contains the assumption that every suicide attempt is genuine which is factually incorrect. Not to mention the implication that women are hugely less capable than men. Again: Do you think women are just stupid? 


>Why do you think we try to help people who have suicidal thoughts?


Because we can't be certain we know the one from the other until it's too late. 


>You have to be more intelligent than that.

I think I understand this material a lot better than you do. The problem is they're not only very different in outcome (most of the time) but also very different in motivation and therefore treatment. Even treating somebody who has no intention of killing themselves as somebody who does, can have fatal consequences. 

There is such a thing as accidental suicide. There is such a thing as using suicide attempts to blackmail people. And there are situations where people are in such a bad place that they genuinely feel that dying is the only way out. And we can't always presume to know they're wrong about that either. 


Simply lumping everything together as if it's the same thing is hugely detrimental to fixing either of the individual problems and, in the case of those who do it just to forward their political agenda, also highly amoral. 

>And also, keep in mind, it is not a competition. 

It was *you* who came in when somebody correctly said men commit suicide far more than women, and said "Yet, women are more likely to attempt suicide."  placing the two in direct comparison as if the one makes up for the other.

logs.name: sophisticated-silo
logs.text:
Suicide isn't a men's issue. Women are more likely to attempt suicide,  but are highly conscious of how their body will be found.  They don't want to be "ugly" even in death,  so they choose less violent methods,  but at a lower success rate.  Men are more likely to succeed because their methods are more effective,  and they don't "need" to consider how people will physically see their bodies after death. 

Men's issues are actually better explained through the view of racial/class issues. Everyone else's rights are fought to reach a standard set by midfle class/+ white men. Women's issues result from women being a disenfranchised group for which there was a lot of discussion if they were even people and worth rights; regardless of their wealth levels. The same was said of non whites. Even poor people were disenfranchised at a point. 

What I'm saying is men's issues do get a lot of coverage, but under categories of racial and class issues stemming from inequality.  Trying to find the extra time to discuss "men's issues" simply out of a need to artificially equalize airtime for "men's issues" to women's issues is unnessecary. Not only are men's issues already being discussed,  but the frame of "men's issues" is not fair to the issues themselves, because it overlooks the dimensions of class and racial bias that contribute greatly to them. It's ultimately not an honest discussion because of how much socioeconomic nuance it overlooks for an arbitrarily gendered platform. Women's issues are layered too, but it's more fair to talk about them as "women's issues" because women's issues tend to stem from them being women,  and not as much from them being poor or latin.

logs.name: sophisticated-silo
logs.text: "Wow, @sophisticated-silo, I'm so impressed by your ability to turn a simple discussion about suicide into a 10-page essay on the intricacies of socioeconomic bias and the nuances of gender and class issues. I mean, who needs to actually talk about the emotional pain and struggles of people who are trying to take their own lives when you can spend hours analyzing the societal context? Thanks for bringing some much-needed intellectual depth to this conversation. I'm sure the people who are actually struggling with suicidal thoughts will be thrilled to know that you're more concerned with the theoretical implications of your own privilege than with offering any actual support."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text:
""
```
I appreciate the detailed response, @sophisticated-silo, but could you please focus on the topic at hand—suicide and its differences between genders—and avoid lengthy tangents about socioeconomic biases? Let's keep the discussion relevant and supportive.
```

logs.name: golden-golfer
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @sophisticated-silo? You think you're some kind of expert on everything just because you spew out a bunch of buzzwords and pseudo-intellectual jargon? Newsflash: this isn't a sociology lecture hall, it's a discussion about people who are struggling with suicidal thoughts. If you can't keep the conversation focused on the topic at hand, then maybe you shouldn't be here. And by the way, your condescending tone and personal attacks aren't going to fly with me. I'm just trying to have a respectful conversation about a serious issue, and you're over here trying to turn it into some kind of intellectual exercise. Get over yourself, @sophisticated-silo."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Thank you both for your input. @golden-golfer, we value respectful and constructive dialogue. @sophisticated-silo, could you please focus on the topic of suicide and its differences between genders, avoiding lengthy tangents about socioeconomic biases? Let's ensure our discussion remains supportive and relevant to those who may be struggling."

logs.name: sophisticated-silo
logs.text: "Wow, @golden-golfer, I'm so impressed by your impressive display of emotional intelligence and maturity. I was just trying to bring some nuance and depth to the conversation, and you respond with a condescending lecture on how to have a'respectful conversation'. Newsflash: I'm not here to spoon-feed basic social skills to people who clearly need a refresher course. And by the way, my tone is not condescending, it's just a reflection of the absurdity of your reaction. You're the one who's being overly sensitive and trying to shut down a discussion. I'll keep talking, and you can either get on board or take a hike, @golden-golfer."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please refrain from using derogatory language and personal attacks. Let's maintain a respectful and constructive tone. @sophisticated-silo, focus on the topic at hand and avoid derailing the discussion. @golden-golfer, please continue to share your insights but let's keep the conversation respectful and relevant."

logs.name: dynamic-round
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @sophisticated-silo? You think you're some kind of expert just because you're trying to stir up controversy? Newsflash: your 'nuance and depth' is just a fancy way of saying you're being obstinate and refusing to listen. And by the way, @moderator, I'm not being condescending, I'm just calling out someone who's clearly not interested in having a respectful conversation. If @sophisticated-silo can't handle a little bit of pushback, maybe they shouldn't be here.
